old_value: {"id":"ox-ok3","title":"Fix concurrent agent recording race condition","description":"Ghost-clearing logic in StartRecording, runAgentSessionStart, and startSessionRecording treats recordings from different agent IDs as ghosts and deletes them, destroying active recordings from concurrent agents sharing the same XDG cache (same repo ID). Fix: add agent-aware recording state functions and update all agent-context callers.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-05T20:11:21Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-05T20:11:21Z"}

new_value: Implemented: 5 agent-aware functions in recording.go, fixed all agent-context callers across 11 files, added 4 concurrent agent regression tests. All 5761 tests pass, 0 lint issues.
{"status":"in_progress"}
old_value:
{"id":"ox-n6f","title":"Fix stale file paths in session stop output after ledger upload","description":"After successful ledger upload, ox agent session stop prunes the local cache (os.RemoveAll) but returns JSON output with raw_path, events_path, html_path, summary_md_path, session_md_path still pointing to the deleted cache directory. The summary_prompt (built before pruning) also embeds the stale cache path. This causes agents to search for non-existent files and incorrectly conclude 'the session recording file wasn't written to disk.' Observed in test-public repo where a valid session with entries was processed and uploaded successfully, but the agent couldn't find the files afterward.

Two fixes needed:

1. CLI (cmd/ox/agent_session.go ~line 947): After successful cache pruning, clear stale paths in result (RawPath, EventsPath, HTMLPath, SummaryMDPath, SessionMDPath, PlanPath) or replace them with ledger paths.

2. summary_prompt path: BuildSummaryPrompt is called at line 838 (before upload/pruning) and embeds result.RawPath. Either rebuild the prompt after upload with the ledger path, or ensure the prompt uses ledger_session_dir when available. The skill (extensions/claude/commands/ox-session-stop.md lines 14-15) instructs agents to read from the path in summary_prompt, which is stale.

Key files:
- cmd/ox/agent_session.go (processAgentSession, ~lines 838-950)
- internal/session/summarize.go (BuildSummaryPrompt)
- extensions/claude/commands/ox-session-stop.md","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-05T22:54:17Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-05T22:54:17Z"}

new_value: Fixed: after cache prune, rewrite result paths to ledger equivalents and rebuild summary prompt

old_value: {"id":"ox-hwe","title":"Track cumulative context consumption per agent instance","description":"Track how many tokens/chars ox commands whisper into each agent's context window on a per-agent-ID basis. Each ox command that produces stdout (prime, query, team-ctx, session list) should add its output size to a running counter in the agent instance record. This enables: (1) visibility into how much context budget ox consumes vs the agent's working memory, (2) ox status/doctor can surface the metric, (3) product decisions about when to compress output harder. The agent instance JSONL already tracks prime_call_count — extend with context_chars or similar.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-06T02:29:46Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-06T02:29:46Z"}

new_value: {"assignee":"Ryan Snodgrass","status":"in_progress"}
old_value: {"id":"ox-nas","title":"Fix ox doctor complaints after fresh ox init","description":"After running ox init in a fresh git repo, ox doctor shows 7 warnings and 1 failure. Root causes: (1) init staging race — files modified after staging, (2) doctor git auth false positive — ox uses PAT auth not credential helper, (3) ledger push failure during daemon bootstrap, (4) noisy post-init warnings. Tests don't catch this because they either cheat (commit before doctor) or filter out every failure.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-06T18:00:10Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:00:10Z"}

new_value: {"description":"New subcommand to add an expert coworker to a team's context repo. Usage: ox coworker add \u003cfilename\u003e [--team \u003cid\u003e]. Workflow: (1) Validate .md file has YAML frontmatter with required 'description' field, warn if 'model' missing. (2) Resolve target team path via FindRepoTeamContext or --team flag. (3) Copy to \u003cteam_path\u003e/coworkers/agents/\u003cname\u003e.md. (4) git add + git commit in the team context repo. Daemon handles push on next sync cycle. Users can also manually git commit coworker files — this command is a convenience wrapper with validation."}
old_value: {"id":"ox-xi5","title":"Add ox coworker add \u003cfilename\u003e command","description":"New subcommand to add an expert coworker to a team. Usage: ox coworker add reviewer.md [--team \u003cid\u003e]. Validates .md frontmatter (description, optional model), resolves target team path via FindRepoTeamContext or --team flag, copies to \u003cteam_path\u003e/coworkers/agents/\u003cname\u003e.md, confirms success.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-06T18:40:04Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:40:04Z"}

new_value: {"description":"New subcommand to remove an expert coworker from a team's context repo. Usage: ox coworker remove \u003cname\u003e [--team \u003cid\u003e] [--force]. Takes coworker name (not filename). Prompts for confirmation unless --force. Deletes \u003cteam_path\u003e/coworkers/agents/\u003cname\u003e.md, then git add + git commit the deletion in the team context repo. Daemon handles push on next sync cycle."}
old_value: {"id":"ox-8mx","title":"Add ox coworker remove \u003cname\u003e command","description":"New subcommand to remove an expert coworker from a team. Usage: ox coworker remove \u003cname\u003e [--team \u003cid\u003e] [--force]. Takes coworker name (not filename). Prompts for confirmation unless --force. Deletes \u003cteam_path\u003e/coworkers/agents/\u003cname\u003e.md.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-06T18:40:05Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:40:05Z"}

{"notes":"Part of epic ox-2wd: Preserve dirty working tree during blue-green GC reclone"}
old_value:
{"id":"ox-43d","title":"Push unpushed local commits before GC reclone","description":"Before running blue-green GC, check for unpushed local commits with 'git rev-list @{u}..HEAD'. If any exist (from ox import, ox remember, etc.), push them to remote before proceeding with reclone. If push fails, abort GC for this cycle rather than destroying committed work.

Location: runBlueGreenGC() in internal/daemon/sync.go, before the isCheckoutClean() check.

Steps:
1. Run 'git rev-list @{u}..HEAD --count' to detect unpushed commits
2. If count > 0, attempt 'git push' with credentials
3. If push succeeds, continue with GC
4. If push fails, return gcSkipped with clear error message (do NOT destroy unpushed commits)
5. Surface the issue via daemon issue tracker so ox doctor/ox status show it","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:16Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:16Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Part of epic ox-2wd: Preserve dirty working tree during blue-green GC reclone"}
old_value:
{"id":"ox-6cx","title":"Preserve uncommitted tracked file changes during GC reclone","description":"Instead of skipping GC entirely when dirty files exist, preserve modified tracked files and carry them into the fresh clone.

Location: runBlueGreenGC() in internal/daemon/sync.go, replacing the isCheckoutClean() skip.

Steps:
1. Run 'git status --porcelain' to get list of modified tracked files (M, A, D prefixes)
2. Before swap: copy modified working tree files from old clone to a temp staging area
3. Run the reclone (TwoPhaseClone into .new path)
4. After swap: copy preserved files from staging area into the new clone
5. Log which files were preserved for debugging

Important: Do NOT auto-commit these files. They should remain as uncommitted local changes in the new clone, exactly as they were in the old one. The user's in-progress work should be seamless.

Edge case: if a preserved file conflicts with a file from the fresh clone (e.g., remote updated the same file), keep BOTH — the user's version as the working tree file, the remote version as committed. This matches normal git behavior.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:23Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:23Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Part of epic ox-2wd: Preserve dirty working tree during blue-green GC reclone"}
old_value:
{"id":"ox-7cv","title":"Preserve untracked files during GC reclone","description":"Untracked files (not in .gitignore) should be carried from the old clone into the new clone during blue-green GC.

Location: runBlueGreenGC() in internal/daemon/sync.go.

Steps:
1. Run 'git ls-files --others --exclude-standard' to get untracked files (respects .gitignore)
2. Copy these files to a temp staging area before swap
3. After swap: copy them into the new clone at the same relative paths
4. Log which untracked files were preserved

Note: gitignored files (like .sageox/cache/, .sageox/checkout.json) should NOT be preserved — they are regenerated by the daemon. Only files that would show up in 'git status' as untracked should be carried over.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:28Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:28Z"}

new_value: {"notes":"Part of epic ox-2wd: Preserve dirty working tree during blue-green GC reclone"}
old_value:
{"id":"ox-vg9","title":"Add regression tests for dirty-file preservation during GC","description":"Write tests that verify dirty working tree state is preserved across blue-green GC reclone.

Test cases:
1. Modified tracked file survives GC reclone (e.g., user edited docs/architecture.md)
2. New untracked file survives GC reclone (e.g., user created docs/new-guide.md)
3. Unpushed local commits are pushed before GC proceeds
4. GC aborts if unpushed commit push fails (no data loss)
5. Gitignored files are NOT preserved (daemon cache is regenerated)
6. Staged-but-uncommitted changes: document behavior (index state may not survive, but working tree files do)

Location: internal/daemon/sync_test.go or new file internal/daemon/gc_test.go.

Use existing test helpers: create bare+clone repos, enable sparse-checkout, simulate dirty state, trigger GC, verify files survive.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ryan@sageox.ai","created_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:33Z","created_by":"Ryan Snodgrass","updated_at":"2026-03-06T20:20:33Z"}
